confirmed_facts: @coze/api version in the inspected mini-program repository is 1.3.9.
RunWorkflowData contains data, cost, token, msg, debug_url and execute_id.
The official SDK history method requires both workflowId and executeId.
The SDK history endpoint is GET /v1/workflows/{workflowId}/run_histories/{executeId}.
The mini-program wrapper returns only response.data and does not persist or return execute_id, debug_url, cost or token.
Local project logs and archived evidence contain no workflow execute_id for the target window.
The Coze canvas exposes a history button, but the read-only browser connection could not complete opening the history detail after two bounded attempts.

module_result.reason: execute_id was not retained and no independently queryable historical record was recovered
execute_id was not retained and no independently queryable historical record was recovered
execute_id was not retained and no independently queryable historical record was recovered

conclusion: The 19:33-19:34 historical executions cannot be reconstructed from the official workflow history API because the required execute_id was discarded by the client wrapper and was not archived elsewhere. This is a confirmed observability/data-association gap, not proof that the workflows did or did not execute.
